items.title: Measure before adding dimensions
items.avoid: Blindly concatenating embeddings, hashes, and one-hot expansions without validation.
items.prefer: Track validation metric vs feature count; ablate new column groups on the same split.
items.why: Each independent direction worsens sparsity and distance concentration — prove marginal value per added d.
items.example: A/B: baseline 20 features vs +100 hash buckets; keep hashes only if AUC improves on held-out month.

items.title: Diagnose distance concentration empirically
items.avoid: Assuming k-NN failure is only wrong k or missing scaling.
items.prefer: Plot distance histograms to a few queries; compute max/min ratio across training set as d changes.
items.why: Shrinking max/min ratio signals concentration — motivates reduction before hyperparameter sweeps.
items.example: d=100 random noise: max/min ≈ 1.05; d=5 informative: max/min ≈ 3+ — neighbor gaps still separable.

items.title: Reduce intrinsic dimension when n ≪ d
items.avoid: Training distance-based models in full gene-expression or pixel space with hundreds of rows.
items.prefer: PCA, supervised selection, or domain embeddings to 10–50 components when n is small — see PCA topic.
items.why: Sample complexity in ambient d is exponential; reduction trades bias for usable local density.
items.example: n=200, d=20,000 genes → PCA(30) + k-NN beats raw k-NN in cross-validation.

items.title: Match model class to geometry
items.avoid: Defaulting to k-NN for every tabular problem regardless of d and n.
items.prefer: Use local methods when d is moderate and n supports neighborhoods; prefer trees, linear + regularization, or kernels with structure in high ambient d.
items.why: The curse hits local evidence hardest — global parametric bias can generalize with fewer neighbors per cell.
items.example: Same fraud set: k-NN weak at d=300; gradient-boosted trees competitive without explicit reduction.

items.title: Scale and deduplicate features
items.avoid: Leaving duplicate or wildly unscaled columns that inflate effective volume without information.
items.prefer: Remove exact duplicates, standardize continuous columns, group correlated sensors before distance work.
items.why: Redundant axes artificially increase d and distort concentration without adding independent signal.
items.example: Drop second copy of 'amount_usd'; StandardScaler on remaining numerics before distance diagnostics.
